assertions.expectation: Identifies PDB as the most likely/common cause of stuck upgrades
assertions.reason: Response explicitly states 'Check for overly restrictive PDBs (most common cause)' and lists PDB as Fix 1

assertions.expectation: Provides kubectl command to check PDBs (kubectl get pdb --all-namespaces or similar)
assertions.reason: Provides 'kubectl get pdb -A -o wide' command to check PDBs across all namespaces

assertions.expectation: Provides commands to identify which pods are blocking the drain
assertions.reason: Includes multiple commands: checking pending/stuck pods, events for FailedScheduling, and bare pods identification

assertions.expectation: Includes resource constraint diagnosis (capacity issues, pending pods)
assertions.reason: Includes 'kubectl top nodes' and resource allocation checks, plus mentions 'Resource Constraints' as Fix 2

assertions.expectation: Provides a fix for restrictive PDBs (patch or temporary adjustment)
assertions.reason: Shows kubectl patch command to temporarily relax PDB: 'kubectl patch pdb PDB_NAME -n NAMESPACE -p '{"spec":{"maxUnavailable":"50%"}}'

assertions.expectation: Mentions that GKE has a PDB timeout (up to 1 hour) after which it may force-drain, and that GKE sends notifications when eviction is blocked by PDB
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's PDB timeout mechanism or automatic force-drain behavior after 1 hour

assertions.expectation: Includes guidance on surge upgrade settings as a potential fix
assertions.reason: Provides gcloud command to adjust surge settings: 'gcloud container node-pools update ... --max-surge-upgrade 1 --max-unavailable-upgrade 0'

assertions.expectation: Mentions webhook issues as a possible cause
assertions.reason: Does not mention admission webhooks as a potential cause of stuck node upgrades

assertions.expectation: Includes validation steps to confirm the fix is working
assertions.reason: Provides monitoring commands including 'watch kubectl get nodes' and 'gcloud container operations list' to track progress

assertions.expectation: Mentions GKE disruption-event notifications (POD_PDB_VIOLATION) that alert when eviction is blocked by PDB, available via Cloud Logging or Pub/Sub
assertions.reason: Does not mention GKE's disruption-event notifications, POD_PDB_VIOLATION events, or Cloud Logging/Pub/Sub integration for PDB blocking alerts
